description: neighborhood of Los Angeles, California, United States
description_wiki: Sawtelle  is a neighborhood in West Los Angeles, on the Westside of Los Angeles, California.

description: governing body of the City of Los Angeles, CA, US
description_wiki: The Los Angeles City Council is the lawmaking body for the city government of Los Angeles, California, the second largest city in the United States.

description: neighborhood in the northern central portion of the Westside region of Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: Westwood is a commercial and residential neighborhood in the northern central portion of the Westside region of the city of Los Angeles, California.

description: neighborhood on the Westside of Los Angeles, California, United States
description_wiki: Bel Air is a residential neighborhood in the Westside region on the foothills of the Santa Monica Mountains of Los Angeles, California, United States.

description: affluent neighborhood in the Westside of Los Angeles, California, United States
description_wiki: Brentwood is a primarily residential neighborhood in the City of Los Angeles, part of the Westside area of the Los Angeles Basin.

description: neighborhood in Los Angeles, California, United States
description_wiki: Pacific Palisades is a neighborhood in the Westside region of the city of Los Angeles, California, situated about 20 miles (32 km) west of downtown.

description: second generation of Japanese immigrants
description_wiki: Nisei (二世, "second generation") is a Japanese-language term used in countries in North America and South America to specify the ethnically Japanese children born in the new country to Japanese-born immigrants, or Issei.

description: Term for Japanese Americans
description_wiki: Kibei (kibei (帰米; literally "go home to America")) refers to Japanese Americans born in the United States whose parents had sent them to receive their education in Japan and who had then returned to the US by the time that the US and Japan became engaged in war in the 1940s.

description: Japanese people who were the first generation of immigrants from Japan
description_wiki: Issei (一世, "first generation") are Japanese immigrants to countries in North America and South America.

description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Hiroshima Prefecture (広島県, Hiroshima-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [çi.ɾo.ɕi.ma, -maꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūgoku region of Honshu.

description: organization
description_wiki: The Japanese American Citizens League (日系アメリカ人市民同盟, Nikkei Amerikajin Shimin Dōmei) is an Asian American civil rights charity, headquartered in San Francisco, with regional chapters across the United States.

description: World War II Japanese-American internment camp in California
description_wiki: Manzanar is the site of one of ten American concentration camps, where more than 120,000 Japanese Americans were incarcerated during World War II, from March 1942 to November 1945.

description: detainee camp in Arizona, United States
description_wiki:
The Poston Internment Camp, located in Yuma County (now in La Paz County) in southwestern Arizona, was the largest (in terms of area) of the 10 American concentration camps operated by the War Relocation Authority during World War II.
The site was composed of three separate camps arranged in a chain from north to south, three miles from each other.

description: Japanese people who were the first generation of immigrants from Japan
description_wiki: Issei (一世, "first generation") are Japanese immigrants to countries in North America and South America.

description: seat of San Diego County, California, United States; second-largest city in California
description_wiki: San Diego (  SAN dee-AY-goh; Spanish: [san ˈdjeɣo]) is a city on the Pacific coast of Southern California, adjacent to the Mexico–United States border.

description: cinematographer
description_wiki: Takashi Fujimoto ASC (born July 12, 1939) is a retired American cinematographer, known for his frequent collaborations with directors Jonathan Demme and M. Night Shyamalan.

aliases: War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea
description: war between North and South Korea, 1950–1953
description_wiki: The Korean War (25 June 1950 – 27 July 1953) was an armed conflict the Korean Peninsula fought between North Korea (Democratic People's Republic of Korea; DPRK) and South Korea (Republic of Korea; ROK) and their allies.

description: detainee camp in Arizona, United States
description_wiki:
The Poston Internment Camp, located in Yuma County (now in La Paz County) in southwestern Arizona, was the largest (in terms of area) of the 10 American concentration camps operated by the War Relocation Authority during World War II.
The site was composed of three separate camps arranged in a chain from north to south, three miles from each other.

description: infantry regiment of the United States Army
description_wiki: The 442nd Infantry Regiment was an infantry regiment of the United States Army.

description: infantry battalion
Dutch sculptor (born 1960)

surface_forms: Tule Lake became a segregation camp
description: national monument in California
description_wiki: The Tule Lake War Relocation Center, also known as the Tule Lake Segregation Center, was an American concentration camp located in Modoc and Siskiyou counties in California and constructed in 1942 by the United States government to incarcerate Japanese Americans, forcibly removing them from their homes on the West Coast of the United States.

aliases: University of California at Los Angeles
State Normal School at Los Angeles
description: public university in Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: The University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) is a public land-grant research university in the Westwood neighborhood of Los Angeles, California, United States.

description: public university in Berkeley, California, USA
description_wiki: The University of California, Berkeley (UC Berkeley, Berkeley, Cal, or California) is a public land-grant research university in the Southside and Northside neighborhoods of Berkeley, California, United States.

description: US commission established in 1946 in accordance with a presidential directive from Harry S. Truman
description_wiki: The Atomic Bomb Casualty Commission (ABCC) (Japanese:原爆傷害調査委員会, Genbakushōgaichōsaiinkai) was a commission established in 1946 in accordance with a presidential directive from Harry S. Truman to the National Academy of Sciences-National Research Council to conduct investigations of the late effects of radiation among the atomic-bomb survivors in Hiroshima and Nagasaki.

description: capital city of Hiroshima Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Hiroshima (広島市, Hiroshima-shi; English: , also UK: , US: , Japanese: [çiɾoɕima] ) is the capital of Hiroshima Prefecture in Japan.
